author: Dr. Jennifer Adams
content:
TELEPHONE ENCOUNTER — 10/2/2025, 3:22 PM

Patient: Robert Morrison (MRN: MRN-78432156)
DOB: 3/15/1952 (Age 73)
Callback number verified: (555) 482-7136

REASON FOR CALL:
Patient's wife Patricia called to report mild increase in bilateral leg swelling over the past 3 days. She is monitoring his daily weights as instructed.

CLINICAL DISCUSSION:
Spoke with Mrs. Morrison first, then patient directly. Mrs. Morrison reports that her husband's ankle swelling has been noticeably more prominent for the past 3 days, with his socks leaving deeper impressions than usual. She has been tracking his daily weights:
- 9/29: 98.5 kg
- 9/30: 98.8 kg
- 10/1: 99.1 kg
- 10/2: 99.0 kg (this morning)

This represents approximately a 1.5 kg (3.3 lb) increase from his recent baseline of ~97.5 kg. The increase coincided with a family gathering last weekend where the patient admits he ate more liberally than usual, including his daughter-in-law's pasta casserole and salted snacks. He has otherwise been compliant with his diuretics (furosemide 60mg AM + 40mg PM) and all other medications.

Patient reports his breathing is at baseline — can walk about 2 blocks without stopping. No increase in dyspnea at rest, no orthopnea (still 2 pillows), no PND. No chest pain. No new cough. He reports his urine output has been somewhat less over the past 2 days compared to usual. Neuropathy symptoms at baseline.

He denies any fever, chills, or unilateral leg swelling that might suggest DVT. No redness, warmth, or pain in either leg beyond the swelling. His blood pressure at home was 142/84 this morning (home cuff, left arm, seated).

Given the clinical picture — modest weight gain attributable to dietary sodium indiscretion, bilateral symmetric edema without signs of acute decompensation (no dyspnea worsening, no orthopnea, no resting tachycardia) — this appears to be mild volume overload that may respond to dietary re-restriction and possibly a short-term diuretic boost.

PLAN:
- Take one additional furosemide 20mg dose today (on top of scheduled doses) — extra dose to be taken at 2 PM to avoid nighttime diuresis given his existing nocturia and fall risk
- Strict sodium restriction <2g/day for the next 5-7 days — Mrs. Morrison will prepare all meals
- Weigh daily and log values; call back if weight does not trend down within 48 hours or if it exceeds 100 kg
- Elevate legs above heart level when sitting — 30 minutes, 3 times daily
- Continue all regular medications as prescribed
- Monitor for worsening: increasing SOB, inability to lie flat, productive cough with frothy sputum, weight gain >3 lbs in a single day, or any chest pain — any of these should prompt ED evaluation
- If weight continues to trend down over 48-72 hours, no further action needed beyond dietary adherence
- If weight remains elevated or symptoms worsen, will see patient in office for examination and possible diuretic adjustment
- Furosemide asymmetric dosing schedule may need revision at next office visit — currently 60mg AM + 40mg PM, may increase PM dose to 60mg if pattern recurs

Note: Discussed with Mrs. Morrison the importance of meal preparation oversight, as prior HF exacerbation (August 2022) occurred when she was away and patient was managing independently. She acknowledges and confirms she will be home for the foreseeable future.

October labs due — will order BMP, CBC, BNP to assess current status. Labs to be drawn at outpatient lab this week.

Call duration: 15 minutes. Spoke with wife and patient. Both verbalized understanding of plan.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Jennifer Adams
Date: 10/2/2025
